title: tephrosia bracteolata var. bracteolata
intro: Tephrosia bracteolata var. bracteolata, a plant species commonly known as the "White Serpent Creeper," is a perennial climber native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial infections, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia bracteolata var. bracteolata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues.
